senses.definition: a part of something that has been reconstituted or restored to its original form
examples: The reconstituent was added to the sample to restore its composition.
In the lab, the reconstituent helped bring the powder back to a usable solution.

senses.definition: plural of reconstituent; components that have been restored or brought back to a previous state
examples: The reconstitutents are mixed to form the final compound.
Researchers analyzed the reconstitutents before proceeding.

senses.definition: able to be reconstituted or restored to a previous form or state
examples: The dried powder is reconstitutable with water.
Only certain compounds are reconstitutable in this process.

senses.definition: to restore something to its former condition or composition, often by adding water or other substance
examples: We reconstitute the dried soup with hot water.
The archivists reconstituted the old manuscript from fragments.

senses.definition: restored to its original form or composition
examples: The reconstituted soup was ready to serve.
After drying, the reconstituted powder dissolved easily.

senses.definition: he/she/it restores something to its former condition or makes it usable again
examples: The agent reconstitutes the powdered drink with water.
Researchers reconstitutes the data from archived records.

senses.definition: the process of restoring something to its former state
examples: The team is reconstituting the powder for examination.
Reconstituting the culture requires careful handling.

senses.definition: the act of restoring something to its former state; the process of reconstituting
examples: The reconstitution of the sample took several hours.
Reconstitution is required before the enzyme can be measured.

senses.definition: plural of reconstitution; instances or processes of restoring something
examples: The lab documented several reconstitutions of the compound.
Historical reconstitutions help explain the original practices.

senses.definition: to build or assemble again something that was broken or destroyed
examples: The archaeologists tried to reconstruct the ancient city from ruins.
We can reconstruct the car from the pieces we found.

senses.definition: built again after being destroyed or damaged
examples: The reconstructed bridge opened last week.
A reconstructed portrait hangs in the museum.

senses.definition: capable of being reconstructed
examples: The data were found to be reconstructible from partial records.
Not all fragments are reconstructible.

senses.definition: the process of rebuilding or restoring something that was damaged
examples: They are reconstructing the city after the earthquake.
The researchers are reconstructing the evolutionary history.

senses.definition: the act of reconstructing; the process of rebuilding or restoring
examples: Post-war reconstruction took many years.
The reconstruction of the data required careful verification.

senses.definition: relating to reconstruction or the process of reconstructing
examples: The reconstructional analysis helped guide the project.
They discussed reconstructional methods in the meeting.

senses.definition: relating to reconstruction or the theory of reconstructing
examples: The reconstructionary theory influenced archaeological methods.
They studied reconstructionary approaches to history.

senses.definition: a theory or movement emphasizing reconstruction or revival of a culture, language, or system
examples: Reconstructionism argues for revived traditional practices.
Cultural reconstructionism influenced the curriculum.

senses.definition: plural of reconstructionism; movements or theories of reconstruction
examples: Various reconstructionisms emerged in the late century.
Scholars compared different reconstructionisms.

senses.definition: a person who advocates or studies reconstructionism
examples: The reconstructionist argued for linguistic revival.
Several reconstructionists participated in the symposium.

senses.definition: plural of reconstructionist; people who advocate reconstructionism
examples: The group included several reconstructionists.
Reconstructionists debated methodologies.

senses.definition: the act or result of reconstructing; restored versions or explanations
examples: Several reconstructions of the event exist.
The reconstructions helped historians visualize the scene.

senses.definition: serving to reconstruct or restore; intended to repair damage
examples: Reconstructive surgery aims to restore function and appearance.
They studied reconstructive techniques in medicine.

senses.definition: in a way that reconstructs or restores
examples: The team worked reconstructively to rebuild the dataset.
Procedures were applied reconstructively to improve accuracy.

senses.definition: the quality of being reconstructive; the ability to restore or rebuild
examples: The reconstructiveness of the method impressed the panel.
A high level of reconstructiveness is desired in the protocol.

senses.definition: a person or device that reconstructs; someone who rebuilds or restores
examples: The reconstructor pieced the model together.
A surgical reconstructor assisted during the operation.
